title: audit — model relationships
description: Two owned entities with sparse relations — Audit is a leaf in the graph (its only outbound FKs are polymorphic to auditable + user; those are morphs, not enforced FKs). AuditRetentionPolicy belongs to Tenant. Cross-module: every audited model implicitly relates to Audit rows via polymorphic morphMany, but that reverse relation is provided by the vendor `Auditable` trait — not declared here.

outbound.description: Every audit row is tenant-scoped. On tenant erasure the row is NOT cascade-deleted — audit rows MIGRATE to a compliance archive per the PurgeAuditDataForErasedTenantHook. Regulators may need proof of erasure years later, and audit rows are the record of what happened before + during the erasure.
Nullable — platform-plane audits have no application context. Set-null on application delete so the audit row survives an application decommission (rare).

outbound.to: auditable (any model composing the vendor Auditable trait)
outbound.description: Polymorphic subject. Every module that adds the vendor Auditable trait becomes a valid morph target: Membership, TenantSubscription, Athlete, Team, etc. The morph is INFORMATIONAL only — a soft reference. When the underlying subject is deleted, the audit row survives (the deletion itself created a `deleted` audit row).

Polymorphic actor. NULL for system-fired mutations (queue jobs without a user context). PlatformUser::class for platform-admin actions. ServiceAccount::class for machine-to-machine mutations. User::class for the standard tenant-actor path.
Retention policies are strictly tenant-scoped. On tenant erasure, cascade deletes the policy (the audit rows themselves migrate to the compliance archive; the policy no longer applies).

Export request metadata — tenant-scoped. Cascade delete on tenant erasure removes the request record (the archived export payload in S3 is deleted separately by PurgeExpiredAuditsJob at retention boundary).
Requester. Nullable because platform-admin exports are attributed to requested_by_platform_admin_id instead.

inbound.description: Every audited model implicitly gets a morphMany relation to Audit rows via the vendor `Auditable` trait's `audits()` method — this reverse relation is provided by the vendor package, not declared in this module. Consumers query with $membership->audits()->orderByDesc('created_at')->limit(20)->get() etc.
consumer_patterns: any Eloquent model composing OwenIt\Auditing\Auditable and implementing OwenIt\Auditing\Contracts\Auditable — gets $model->audits() morphMany automatically
any queue subscribed to AuditRecorded (via listeners.json here or in another module) — receives the event with tenant_id + auditable_type + auditable_id + user_type + user_id + event
consumer_examples: compliance::PublishComplianceMetricsListener — subscribes to AuditRecorded to increment the compliance metrics counter
monitoring::EmitAuditFireRateListener — subscribes to AuditRecorded to emit ops metrics
activity::CorrelateAuditToActivityListener (planned Wave 6) — subscribes to AuditRecorded to correlate an audit row to an activity_log row when both are created in the same request cycle
growth::analytics — does NOT subscribe (analytics is behavioral, audit is compliance; different lanes per hierarchy.md §11)

consumed.usage: Every audit row is tenant-scoped via BelongsToTenant on our Audit model wrapper. Every retention policy belongs to a tenant (cascade delete).

Audit rows carry application_id directly (per tenancy-columns.md §2 — audits is one of 8 named rows that DO carry application_id). NULL for platform-plane audits. Populated from request context (X-Application-Id middleware) when caller has one.
Polymorphic actor on Audit. NULL for system-fired. Populated by the vendor observer from Auth::user() at the time of the mutation.
Polymorphic actor on Audit for platform-admin actions.
Polymorphic actor on Audit for machine-to-machine mutations (e.g. Stripe webhook processing that fires our Auditable trait).

audit_capture (all-tier master gate). audit_export (Medium+, self-service DSAR). audit_retention_extended (Enterprise, extend past 7y to 10y). audit_regulator_export (Enterprise, cross-application regulator export).
Three notification categories seeded under the `audit` namespace for retention-policy, purge, and export lifecycle notifications.

NOT consumed directly. Audit is NEVER consent-gated. Cross-reference is one-way: consent operations THEMSELVES generate audit rows (a ConsentGranted / ConsentRevoked mutation writes audit rows via the vendor Auditable trait on the ConsentRecord model).
Third-party dependency. Provides the Auditable trait, the AuditableObserver, and the base Audit model shape. This module extends the vendor schema + wraps the model.
